items.title: Policy paper: Life Sciences Sector Plan
items.summary: The Life Sciences Sector Plan sets out a vision and an action plan to drive growth, innovation, and better health outcomes.

items.title: Policy paper: Life Sciences Sector Plan: One Year On
items.summary: Progress report on delivering the Life Sciences Sector Plan.

items.title: Sizewell B power plant given lifetime extension to 2055
items.summary: Sizewell B power plant given 20‑year lifetime extension to continue operating until 2055.

items.title: Official Statistics: UK trade in numbers
items.summary: A snapshot of the UK’s latest trade and investment position, summarising statistics produced by ONS, DBT, and UNCTAD.

items.title: Draft strategic policy guidance for electricity networks growth
items.summary: We're seeking views on draft social and environmental guidance for the Gas and Electricity Markets Authority.

items.title: Guidance: UK-India CETA origin declaration
items.summary: Origin declaration guidance for UK exports under the terms of the UK-India Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA).

items.title: Sussex Innovation Centre (SINC)
items.summary: Tailored support services, events and access to the resources of the University of Sussex to help with innovation projects.

items.title: Government approves UK’s second largest solar farm
items.summary: Families and businesses will benefit from more solar power as the government has approved a major new solar power project.

items.title: Cool Ventures
items.summary: Cool Ventures can supply business advice, workshops and a range of useful links E-learning tutorials via their website.

items.title: Decision: One Earth Solar Farm: development consent order, Planning Act 2008
items.summary: Decision on an application for a solar farm on land surrounding the River Trent in Nottinghamshire and Lincolnshire, under the Planning Act 2008.

items.title: Smart Data: multi-sector call for evidence
items.summary: We're seeking evidence to shape Smart Data schemes across sectors, including use cases, design choices, governance and international best practice.

items.title: Productive Valley Fund
items.summary: Loans of between £50,000 and £400,000, for projects such as premises refurbishment, building works and equipment purchases that will lead to increased productivity, job creation and business growth.

items.title: East Devon Voluntary, Community and Social Enterprise Support Service
items.summary: Free training sessions for organizations either based in or working with communities in East Devon.

items.title: Somerset Business Agency (SBA)
items.summary: Somerset Business Agency (SBA) provide advice, workshops and support to micro-businesses and new start-ups, as well as businesses looking to grow.

items.title: Update following negotiations on enhanced FTA with Turkey
items.summary: An update following round 5 of negotiations on an enhanced Free Trade Agreement between the UK and Turkey.

items.title: British Industrial Competitiveness Scheme: consultation on scheme eligibility and approach
items.summary: We are seeking views on the proposed approach to the new British Industrial Competitiveness Scheme (BICS) and how eligible businesses should be selected.

items.title: British Industrial Competitiveness Scheme: consultation on regulatory changes and scheme delivery
items.summary: This consultation seeks views on the proposed regulatory changes and approach to scheme delivery for the new British Industrial Competitiveness Scheme (BICS).

items.title: Make Work Pay: workplace monitoring technologies
items.summary: We are seeking views on proposals to support the fair, transparent and responsible use of workplace monitoring technologies, used to monitor, manage or make decisions relating to workers.

items.title: UK to become world’s fastest market to commercialise innovation with regulatory shake-up
items.summary: The Government has announced regulatory reforms to help businesses test and commercialise new products faster. Marking one year on from the launch of the Modern Industrial Strategy, the changes will boost investment, support innovation, and build on £380 billion in private investment.

items.title: Research: Regulator performance monitoring
items.summary: Growth goals and key performance indicators (KPIs) from 16 key regulators, updated quarterly as part of the UK government’s Regulatory Action Plan.

items.summary: Project Summary

items.title: Policy paper: Industrial Strategy Quarterly Update: April to June 2026
items.summary: An update on delivery of commitments in the Industrial Strategy.

items.title: Unlocking business: reform driven by you
items.summary: This questionnaire asks businesses large and small to tell government about regulation which is not fit for purpose and unjustifiably inhibits growth, innovation and investment.

items.title: Policy paper: A new approach to ensure regulators and regulation support growth
items.summary: An action plan setting out the government's approach to regulation and regulators

items.title: Guidance: Regulatory Sandboxes: What Businesses Can Expect
items.summary: This page sets out how regulatory sandboxes will work for business.

items.title: Eskdalemuir seismic array: revised approach to managing onshore wind turbine interference
items.summary: Proposals for an updated approach to managing onshore wind turbine interference with the Eskdalemuir seismic array.

items.title: Notice: Longer Duration Energy Storage (LoDES) Demonstration Programme: successful projects
items.summary: Projects which received funding through the Longer Duration Energy Storage Demonstration Programme Stream 1 (Phases 1 and 2) and Stream 2 (Phases 1 and 2).

items.summary: This call for evidence seeks views on how to improve the GB Corporate Power Purchase Agreement market.

items.summary: Road fuel price statistics providing average UK retail 'pump' prices on a weekly basis.

items.title: Research: Ambient gamma radiation dose rates across the UK
items.summary: Data received from fixed and mobile monitors measuring the ambient (background) gamma radiation dose rate across the UK.

items.title: Communities backed to lead local climate and nature action
items.summary: Bradford, Manchester, Bristol, London and Birmingham are the first cities to host annual ‘Energising Britain’ events.

items.summary: Special feature articles on energy issues in the United Kingdom from the June 2026 edition of the quarterly statistical bulletin Energy Trends.

items.summary: Special feature article from the June 2026 edition of Energy trends statistical publication.

items.summary: Environmental regulations and guidance on offshore oil and gas exploration and production offshore gas unloading and storage and offshore carbon dioxide storage activities

items.summary: We're seeking views on several refinements to ensure that the CfD continues to reflect current policy and that the scheme continues to function as originally intended.

items.summary: The Standard Terms and Conditions for use in CfD Allocation Round 8 (AR8).

items.summary: The Contract Allocation Framework sets out the rules for Contracts for Difference (CfD) Allocation Round 8 (AR8) and the eligibility requirements applicants must satisfy.

items.summary: Final statutory notices required to launch Contracts for Difference Allocation Round 8.

items.title: Extended CE marking recognition for Ecodesign regulations
items.summary: We’re seeking views on a proposal to extend recognition of CE marking for ecodesign regulations so that it applies to new measures made under ESPR.

items.summary: Qualitative research into the value of in-use home energy performance data to consumers.
